text:
Done — `parseQuery` is implemented in `parse_query.js`.

The logic: empty input returns `{}`. Otherwise it splits on `&`, and for each part finds the first `=` to separate key from value (no `=` or nothing after it yields value `""`). The first time a key is seen it maps to the string value; on the second occurrence the existing value and the new one become a two-element array; further occurrences push onto that array — preserving order.

I tried to run a verification harness over the spec cases (empty, single, repeated keys, bare key, empty value, mixed), but the `node` command is sitting behind a permission prompt that hasn't been approved, so it didn't execute. If you approve it I'll run the cases and show the output. By inspection each spec case maps correctly: `""→{}`, `"a=1"→{a:"1"}`, `"a=1&a=2&a=3"→{a:["1","2","3"]}`, `"flag"→{flag:""}`, `"k="→{k:""}`.
